modifications: torchvision resnet50 (ImageNet1K_V2 pretrained) with the final fc layer replaced by nn.Linear(2048, 4); full network fine-tuned (no frozen backbone).
class_names_note: This is the ACTUAL order the model was trained with (alphabetical), NOT growth-stage chronological order (seedling->developing->flowering->fruiting). Output logit index i corresponds to class_names[i]. Note the on-disk/training class is spelled 'seeding', not 'seedling'.

normalization.note: Standard ImageNet stats, used because the backbone is ImageNet-pretrained. Inference preprocessing: Resize(256) -> CenterCrop(224) -> ToTensor -> Normalize(mean, std).

training.augmentation: RandomResizedCrop(224, scale=(0.8,1.0)), RandomHorizontalFlip, RandomRotation(15), ColorJitter(brightness=0.2, contrast=0.2, saturation=0.2)

dataset_split_methodology: The notebook loads dataset/<class>/train/, dataset/<class>/valid/, and dataset/<class>/test/ as three SEPARATE datasets (via a custom SplitFolderDataset in dataset_utils.py) instead of pooling all images and re-splitting randomly. Only train/ is used for gradient updates, only valid/ is used for checkpoint/model selection (best val_acc), and test/ is held out entirely until final evaluation. Verified no duplicate-content or near-duplicate (same source photo, different augmentation) images leak across splits, by MD5 hash and by Roboflow source-filename matching across all 982 images. Also verified no class overlap issue after a manual dataset fix on 2026-07-10 that moved 2 mislabeled images from flowering/train to fruiting/train (this checkpoint was trained AFTER that fix).
source_checkpoint: checkpoints/resnet50_best.pt (dict with model_state_dict, class_names, epoch, val_acc; re-exported here as a plain state_dict only)
